title: Xenial Light Turkish Lamb Pide
description: A peppery and light Turkish dish featuring red curry paste and nimono simmer, perfect for a nostalgic meal, this diabetic-friendly pide is a must try.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground lamb and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the red curry paste and cook for 1 minute.
Season the lamb mixture with black pepper.
Place the pide bread on a baking sheet and top each piece with the lamb mixture and crumbled feta cheese.
Bake the pides in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the bread is crispy and the cheese is melted.
Remove the pides from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
